display_information.name: Open Brain
display_information.description: Your second brain on Slack. Remembers conversations, surfaces context, and answers questions grounded in your team's history.
display_information.long_description:
Open Brain is your team's institutional memory — a local-first, profile-aware AI brain that listens to your Slack channels, indexes every conversation with semantic search, and answers questions with citations back to the exact messages it learned from.

It's a read layer over your knowledge. Invite it to a channel and Open Brain quietly captures history into your private Open Brain instance (running on your laptop or a private host — not in someone else's cloud). Then later, DM it or @mention it:

• "What did Brian decide about the migration last sprint?"
• "Remind me what we landed on for VPN rotation?"
• "Who has context on the WebRTC pipeline?"

Open Brain replies with answers grounded in actual Slack messages, with clickable citations back to the source.

**Local-first by design.** Your conversations live in your own Postgres instance. No SaaS dependency. Choose your own LLM provider — OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub Models, AWS Bedrock, or local Ollama.

**Per-workspace isolation.** Each Slack workspace maps to its own brain. tech-screen Slack stays separate from linguado Slack — no comingling.

**Open source:** github.com/NateBJones-Projects/OB1
